content.title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
content.summary:
The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year

Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year.

Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cult

content.title: MoD cancels army training in Kenya over powers to prosecute British soldiers
content.summary:
September exercise called off after Kenya raises concerns over funding and jurisdiction for criminal offences

The British army has been forced to cancel training for its troops in Kenya after the Kenyan government refused to ratify a bilateral defence agreement.

While the UK government wanted to renew the existing agreement, Kenyan lawmakers had sought changes to give local authorities greater powers to hold British soldiers accountable for any offences committed in the country.</

content.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
content.summary:
UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease

A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus.

The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said.

content.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
content.summary:
Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well

One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.

There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to ac

content.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
content.summary:
Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent

Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.

According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximatel

content.title: Mayor shot dead in Mexico months after surviving assassination attempt
content.summary:
Valentín Lavín Romero is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in 2024

A mayor was shot dead on Wednesday in the Mexican state of Morelos after having survived a previous assassination attempt earlier this year. He is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in October 2024.

Valentín Lavín Romero, who was from the Green party, an ally of the ruling leftwing Morena party, was wounded at the end of January when hitmen sh

content.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
content.summary:
Trump’s 50% tariff threat means there will be no ribbon-cutting celebration of Gordie Howe International Bridge

A planned joint US-Canada ceremony on Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods.

In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and da

content.title: Nicaragua’s congress announces ‘work plan’ to suspend elections
content.summary:
US secretary of state says international community should ‘join forces’ against authoritarian Ortega regime

Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections.

The UN and the US strongly condemned Sund

content.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
content.summary:
Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close

Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby.

Researchers from

content.title: Trump announces new tariffs on generic drugs to take effect in 2028 – as it happened
content.summary:
This live blog is now closed.

  • Sign up for US Breaking News email

US gas prices have continued to rise since the conflict with Iran resumed this month. According to the American Aut

content.title: In a first, Chinese woman wins the prestigious Fields Medal
content.summary: Despite criticisms of a brain drain from China to the U.S., the awards come at a time when several high-profile Chinese scholars have returned home, some citing an increasingly hostile academic environment in America.

content.title: U.S. signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia. And, Nolan Wells' independent autopsy results
content.summary: The U.S. has signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. And, the independent autopsy results of Nolan Wells fuel more questions surrounding his cause of death.

content.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia's nuclear deal hinges on the kingdom normalizing Israel ties
content.summary: After a Saudi nuclear power deal raised alarm in Israel, President Trump announced online that the arrangement is subject to the kingdom normalizing relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords.

content.title: U.S. signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
content.summary: The agreement between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

content.title: Former State Department official on the implications of the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal
content.summary: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Daniel Benaim, a former State Department official who worked on Gulf issues, about the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal.

content.title: Ebola death toll in DR Congo surpasses 1,000
content.summary: Congo's Ebola outbreak has crossed the grim 1,000-death threshold, marking the fastest fatal surge in  recorded history of the virus.

content.title: Beloved educator's detention fuels India's biggest protests in years
content.summary: In India, the detention of a beloved educator on hunger strike has turned into its largest anti-government protests in years.

content.title: Trump administration signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
content.summary: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

content.title: Why the U.S. and Saudi Arabia signed a nuclear agreement
content.summary: NPR has confirmed a deal that would allow the U.S. to transfer nuclear technology to Saudi Arabia and allow the country to begin enriching uranium for civilian use.

content.title: What to know about Ukraine's military shakeup
content.summary: After a week of nationwide protests over the direction of Ukraine's military strategy in the ongoing war with Russia, the Ukrainian military has a new commander, Mykhailo Drapatyi. Here's what to know.

content.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
content.summary: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.

content.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
content.summary: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

content.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
content.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

content.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
content.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence".

content.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
content.summary: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.

content.title: Yemen's Houthis attack Saudi tanker as US launches more Iran strikes
content.summary: The Iran-backed group, which previously announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia, also claims to have hit another vessel.

content.title: Teen jailed for exam cheating in Guinea dies after sickle cell complications, father says
content.summary: Mamadou Djouma Bah, 17, was arrested and sentenced to prison for exam fraud earlier this month.

content.title: 'Let's enjoy the ride' says Elon Musk, as AI fears mount
content.summary: Talking to The Economist, Musk said he doesn't care if people hate him and said he was not a racist.

content.title: Nicaragua's legislature debates barring opposition from elections
content.summary: The move comes days after President Daniel Ortega demanded 'a wall, a blockade' against opposition parties.

content.title: Ex-governor convicted of murdering a pregnant student in Kenya
content.summary: Okoth Obado was found guilty of killing Sharon Otieno with whom he had had an affair.

content.title: Is this Italy’s ‘George Floyd moment’?
content.summary: The death of Moroccan-born Abderrahim Fakir after he was restrained by police in Bologna has sparked protests.

content.title: US war on Iran: The $110 billion price tag
content.summary: America's war on Iran has cost the US nearly $110 billion. Now, the Trump administration wants another $67 billion.

content.title: Trump warns of largest strikes on Iran yet as Tehran lashes out across Gulf
content.summary: The US president's threat comes after another day of Iranian attacks on Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan.

content.title: US-Saudi nuclear deal hinges on normalisation with Israel
content.summary: President Trump said a proposed US-Saudi civilian nuclear deal is subject to Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords

content.title: Rally attendee steals the show by mimicking Trump’s gestures
content.summary: A rally attendee seated directly behind Trump drew attention by mimicking the president.

content.title: France wildfires force evacuation of over 20,000 people from tourist town
content.summary: About 800 firefighters and hundreds of gendarmes and soldiers deployed to battle the flames west of Bordeaux.

content.title: Videos show the moment police officer shoots man on street in Wisconsin
content.summary: Outrage is growing after videos show a police officer fatally shooting a man in the US state of Wisconsin.

content.title: White House says will not block Tate brothers’ extradition to UK
content.summary: Social media influencers to be extradited after UK authorities file dozens of new sex crime charges against them.

content.title: Deadly monsoon floods kill at least 40 in India
content.summary: At least 40 people have died and up to 80 remain missing after heavy monsoon rains triggered severe flooding.

content.title: US government cancels subpoena against three New York Times reporters
content.summary: Withdrawal is latest instance of government's efforts to crack down on critical media facing setbacks in court.

content.title: Il governo Lula respinge i nuovi dazi di Trump del 12,5% sul lavoro forzato
content.summary: Il Brasile invoca la legge sulla reciprocità contro gli Usa e annuncia ricorso all'Omc

content.title: Trump, 'da ora ogni danno a navi sarà risarcito con i fondi iraniani'
content.summary: 'Danni potrebbero essere ingenti ma è la soluzione più equa'

content.title: Messico, nuove sanzioni Usa contro il nuovo leader del cartello di Jalisco
content.summary: Coinvolte 55 persone e società, congelati beni per riciclaggio e narcotraffico

content.title: Uruguay, gli omicidi aumentano del 6,6% nel primo semestre dell'anno
content.summary: La crescita per lo spostamento dei gruppi criminali, in calo rapine e violenza di genere

content.title: L'AfD vola a Berlino, per la prima volta avanti nei sondaggi
content.summary: Il partito al 19%, seguito da Cdu e Linke. Ma nessuno vuole allearsi con l'estrema destra

content.title: Casa Bianca, dazi dal 10 al 12,5 per cento su molti partner da domani
content.summary: Non ne saranno interessati i prodotti già soggetti a tariffe specifiche per settore

content.title: Cuba, Caritas avvia la distribuzione degli aiuti umanitari inviati dagli Usa
content.summary: Le prime consegne alle famiglie più vulnerabili intorno all'Avana

content.title: Rischio salmonella, ritirati dai supermercati Usa 19 milioni di uova
content.summary: 'Misura precauzionale per prodotto di due allevamenti in Texas'

content.title: Sisi celebra la rivoluzione di Nasser, 'sforzi per la pace non devono cessare'
content.summary: Colpo di Stato incruento segnò nel 1952 la fine della monarchia filobritannica

content.title: Stime Ue, da blocco price cap sul petrolio -3,5 miliardi per Mosca in un anno
content.summary: Il prezzo del greggio Urals è 65,3 dollari ma verrà mantenuto il tetto di 44,7 dollari

content.title: Indian activist ends 26-day hunger strike, though student protests continue
content.summary: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday after talks with government ministers, but student-led protests over leaked medical entrance exam papers continued, with demonstrators demanding the education minister's resignation and wider reforms.

content.title: Trump announces double digit tariffs on 60 countries over forced labour concerns
content.summary: US President Donald Trump imposed new tariffs of up to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries on Thursday, replacing temporary levies that expire on Friday after the Supreme Court struck down his earlier trade measures. The White House said the duties target countries that have failed to curb imports made with forced labour.

content.title: Ghana needs laws to protect victims of witchcraft accusations
content.summary: In tonight's edition, North Africa is in the grip of a severe heatwave, with temperatures nearing 49 degrees Celsius. Also, Six years after the brutal killing of a 90-year-old woman accused of witchcraft shocked Ghana and the world, activists say justice remains incomplete. And The Kigali Cinema Junction is bringing five days of screenings, workshops and industry discussions to audiences across Kigali.

content.title: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger in Paris: 'There's no single blueprint for Democratic Party'
content.summary: A rising star among Democratic Party moderates, Virginia’s first woman governor Abigail Spanberger was elected the same night last November as Democratic Socialist New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani. In a wide-ranging interview with François Picard, Spanberger outlines a pragmatic vision of politics rooted in coalition building, institutional trust, and solving voters’ everyday concerns through practical governance rather than ideological confrontation. She argues that electoral success begins w

content.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
content.summary: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the Gaza conflict on a pathway to a Palestinian state. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

content.title: Reinstating Fedorov 'only right decision Zelensky can make', MP says
content.summary: Ousted Ukrainian defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov said on Thursday he would ​accept no other government position besides his former job, escalating a political crisis that has gripped Ukraine at a pivotal point in the war with Russia. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ukranian MP Liudmyla Buimister says that reinstating Fedorov "is the only right decision that President Zelensky can make".

content.title: Why Iran is targeting Kuwait's desalination plants as part of its war strategy
content.summary: Iranian strikes targeted Kuwait’s desalination and power plants for the fourth consecutive day on Monday. The attacks are putting unprecedented pressure on a Gulf country heavily dependant on desalination for its water supply. Iran is attempting to wage a “psychological war” on a country with few options for striking back, according to some analysts.

content.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear deal contingent on Riyadh normalising relations with Israel
content.summary: President Donald Trump on Thursday set a new condition to a US-Saudi nuclear deal signed the previous day by insisting the agreement was "totally subject to" the Gulf kingdom joining the Abraham Accords. Riyadh has resisted normalising relations with Israel, especially after the Gaza war, and the new condition throws the US-Saudi deal into turmoil.

content.title: Ecuador’s Carapaz wins Tour de France stage 18, Pogacar loses key climbing teammate
content.summary: Ecuador's ​Richard Carapaz scored a decisive win of the Tour de France at stage 18 on Thursday, securing victory in the final climb to Orcieres-Merlette. While Tadej Pogacar had previously maintained his overall lead, the Slovenian suffered a blow as key teammate Brandon McNulty abandoned the race.

content.title: France expected to name football legend Zinedine Zidane as Les Bleus' new coach
content.summary: The French Football Federation on Thursday said it would unveil France's new coach at a news conference on Tuesday, July 28. Football legend Zinedine Zidane is widely expected to replace Didier Deschamps as the national team's new coach.

content.title: Sweden eases mining permits to cut China minerals reliance
content.summary: Sweden has declared the mining of critical minerals and rare earths a national security interest. This will ease and expedite the permit process. The government said the step was needed to counter dependence on China.

content.title: US-Saudi nuclear deal: How will it impact Middle East, US-Iran war?
content.summary: Critics say the US-Saudi deal could spark a nuclear arms race in the Middle East. It's certainly not making US allies, like Israel and the UAE, happy. And reports suggest the US may already be backtracking on the deal.

content.title: US strikes on southern Iran infrastructure widen war
content.summary: The latest US strikes on tunnels, bridges and other infrastructure further inland from the Strait of Hormuz could indicate that Washington is shifting gears in Iran.

content.title: Trump's refugee policy: Only white South Africans welcome?
content.summary: Trump is spending more money on refugees, but it's mostly white South Africans who benefit. People from crisis-stricken countries are being denied entry. Experts say the credibility of US refugee aid has been damaged.

content.title: Can India's 'Cockroach' protest movement go nationwide?
content.summary: What began as satire on social media has turned into a massive Gen Z-led protest movement driven by anger over corruption and high youth unemployment. There are signs it is finding traction outside of Delhi.

content.title: ECB keeps interest rates unchanged amid Iran war inflation
content.summary: The European Central Bank has kept its base interest rates steady but said it was "closely monitoring" the energy price shock. The impact of the war against Iran on energy costs has pushed inflation over ECB targets.

content.title: Canada-EU: Closer trade ties to counter Trump?
content.summary: Given they share the world's longest land border, Canada and the US have historically enjoyed fruitful trade relations. But with Donald Trump escalating his tariff war, Canada is looking to the EU and further afield.

content.title: Oil prices jump as Hormuz, Red Sea crises deepen
content.summary: Renewed strikes on shipping in the Gulf and the Red Sea have dealt a fresh double blow to global oil markets. As prices climb once again, DW examines what else could keep them elevated.

content.title: EU fines Google €890 million over competition rules
content.summary: The European Commission said it found that Google had preferenced its search engine and app store in ways that unfairly harmed competition.

content.title: Could Iran become America's next 'forever war?'
content.summary: The ceasefire has collapsed, attacks are escalating and shipping in the Strait of Hormuz remains under threat. Is the US drifting into another open-ended conflict with no clear way out?

content.title: Lunar Gateway: Earth’s guard post against asteroids?
content.summary: Humankind’s next space outpost, the lunar Gateway, will serve as a staging point to reach the surface of the Moon. A new ESA-backed study is considering whether it could also be used as a deployment point for planetary defence missions, to intercept asteroids approaching dangerously close to Earth.

content.title: ESA commissions world’s first space debris removal
content.summary: ClearSpace-1 will be the first space mission to remove an item of debris from orbit, planned for launch in 2025. The mission is being procured as a service contract with a startup-led commercial consortium, to help establish a new market for in-orbit servicing, as well as debris removal.

content.title: Hera’s radar CubeSat will peer into asteroid’s heart
content.summary: ESA’s proposed Hera asteroid mission for planetary defence will carry the smallest radar instrument ever flown in space – flying on a separate mini-spacecraft, it will approach two asteroids closer than its mothership and probe deep into their cores.

content.title: LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
content.summary: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes less than a month after researchers found that more than 42 percent of games and other apps available for download on LG's webOS store allow unknown third-parties to route their Internet traffic through a user's TV.

content.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
content.summary: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.

content.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
content.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.

content.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
content.summary: A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.

content.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
content.summary: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies [NASDAQ: ALAR]. The action comes roughly two weeks after KrebsOnSecurity published findings from multiple security firms connecting NetNut to the Popa botnet, a collection of at least two million devices that have been compromised by malicious software wi

content.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
content.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.

content.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
content.summary: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].

content.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
content.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

content.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
content.summary: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

content.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
content.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

content.title: Commercial crude oil inventories increased by 2.0 million barrels
content.summary: For the week ending July 17, 2026, commercial crude oil inventories (excluding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve) increased 2.0 million barrels to 411.7 million barrels, 6% below the previous five-year (2021–2025) average. Gasoline inventories increased 0.8 million barrels, 7% below the five-year average. Distillate inventories increased 1.4 million barrels, 10% below the five-year average. Propane/propylene inventories increased 6.3 million barrels, 34% above the five-year average. Total commerci

content.title: New York imports more electricity from Canada after high-voltage transmission line opens
content.summary: On July 3, 2026, the New York Independent System Operator (NYISO) imported 52 gigawatthours (GWh) of electricity from Canada, the most traded between the two areas since January 2025. Some of the imported electricity flowed along the new Champlain Hudson Power Express (CHPE) transmission line between Quebec, Canada, and New York City, which officially reached commercial operations in May after three years of construction.

content.title: What are tank bottoms?
content.summary: Crude oil inventories held at storage facilities in Cushing, Oklahoma, fell below 20 million barrels during the week ending June 19 until the week ending July 10, according to our Weekly Petroleum Status Report.

content.title: Petroleum markets responded to disruptions in the Middle East in the second quarter
content.summary: Petroleum markets in the second quarter of 2026 (2Q26) were characterized by continued disruptions to international crude oil and petroleum product flows through the Strait of Hormuz, contributing to higher and more volatile crude oil prices through most of the quarter. The disruptions also resulted in international buyers seeking alternative supply sources for petroleum products, driving up U.S. refinery margins, production, and exports.

content.title: Global liquefied natural gas trade volumes reached record high in 2025
content.summary: Global liquefied natural gas (LNG) trade volumes increased 5.4% to a record 56.3 billion cubic feet per day last year (Bcf/d), driven largely by U.S. LNG export capacity expanding to meet growing demand, according to a recent report from the International Group of Liquefied Natural Gas Importers (GIIGNL). Global LNG trade has slowed this year following the closure of the key export route for Qatar, the world's second-largest LNG exporter.

content.title: The United States produced more crude oil than any other country in 2025
content.summary: The United States remained the world's largest crude oil producer in 2025, according to our International Energy Statistics database, extending a streak that began in 2018 when the United States overtook Russia to become the world's leading producer.

content.title: U.S. exports of crude oil and petroleum products reached record in April
content.summary: U.S. petroleum exports reached a record in April as disruptions to international crude oil and refined product flows through the Strait of Hormuz increased global demand for U.S. exports. Exports increased to 13.6 million barrels per day (b/d) in April, 15% more than the previous record set in March.

content.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
content.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total e

content.title: U.S. refining capacity decreased during 2025
content.summary: U.S. operable atmospheric distillation capacity, the primary measure of refinery capacity, totaled 18.2 million barrels per calendar day (b/cd) on January 1, 2026-down over 250,000 b/cd (about 1%) compared with January 1, 2025-according to our latest annual Refinery Capacity Report.

content.title: Metered electricity demand in the New York ISO falls midday because of small-scale solar
content.summary: An increase in electricity generation from small-scale solar in New York has decreased the midday demand for metered electricity, amid overall declining load in the state. The trend is particularly notable in the early spring (March and April), when solar generation has an outsized impact because demand is relatively low and conditions for solar generation are favorable.

content.summary: Oil climbed above $100 a barrel after Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers heightened fears of broader supply disruptions.

content.title: Magnolia to Buy WildFire in $4B Deal
content.summary: 'The transaction supports and reinforces Magnolia's business model' Magnolia said.

content.title: Eni Starts Producing Gas Power at Kazakhstan Hybrid Project
content.summary: 'This milestone marks another major step in the development of Kazakhstan's first large-scale hybrid power plant, integrating solar, gas and future wind generation to ensure decarbonized and reliable electricity supply to KazMunayGas subsidiaries'.

content.title: QatarEnergy Prepares to Extend LNG Force Majeure
content.summary: QatarEnergy is preparing to further extend force majeure on liquefied natural gas shipments through mid-October, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

content.title: Equinor Makes New Oil Discovery Near Johan Castberg
content.summary: Equinor and its partners made a new oil discovery that they are now considering developing via existing infrastructure in the Johan Castberg field on Norway's side of the Barents Sea.

content.title: Repsol Sees Surge in Profit
content.summary: Repsol reported EUR 1.84 billion in adjusted net income for the second quarter, up 207 percent year-on-year due to a spike in oil prices.

content.title: Oil Rockets Towards $100
content.summary: Oil is rocketing towards $100 per barrel, adding more than $14 in just a week.

content.title: TotalEnergies Maintains Increased Dividend Rate
content.summary: TotalEnergies announced a dividend of EUR 0.9 per share for the second quarter, up 5.9 percent from the same three-month period last year, and a new share buyback program of up to $1.5 billion for Q3, as higher oil prices continue to lift earnings.

content.title: Burnham Picks Energy Team
content.summary: UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham has selected his energy team.
